etymology.description: 𡍬 (phonetic) + 貝 (semantic: cowry) -> original form 𧸇 evolved -> reduced 𡍬 to 士 and 四 -> final form 𧶠
etymology.interpretation: The character 𧶠 is a phono-semantic compound where 𡍬 serves as the phonetic component while 貝(bèi, "cowry") signifies the semantic association with trade. Over time, the phonetic component 𡍬 evolved into 士(shì, "scholar") and 四(sì, "four"), leading to the current form 𧶠. Although graphically similar to 賣(mài, "sell"), they are etymologically distinct.
